title: Skill Graph Profile
purpose: Represent a skill as a graph of knowledge, scenarios, rules, tools, routing signals, companion contracts and acceptance gates.
non_goals: force graph-first runtime before readiness gate
accept extracted skill knowledge without review
duplicate domain knowledge owned by another skill
readiness_model: Universal T-level readiness with graph-first runtime enablement only after readiness and runtime policy gates pass.
evidence_model: Skill graph knowledge requires source refs, owner review or benchmark/runtime evidence before graph-first use.
quality_gates: every rule has source or owner
every routing signal maps to owner skill or specialist
extracted skill knowledge is not accepted without review
safety_policy: Skill graph may route and generate context, but graph-first runtime and action suggestions remain policy-gated; dangerous actions require external approval.
retention_policy: Raw skill sources remain in the skill repository; generated context and reports are rebuildable; learning proposals remain draft until accepted.
federation_policy: Skill graph exports bounded summaries for federation and must not duplicate domain knowledge owned by another skill.
migration_policy: Existing SKILL.md, specialists, rules, activities, references, quality gates and examples are indexed into graph candidates, reviewed and accepted incrementally.
